35	\section{Setup and motivation}
36	\label{sec:relpic_setup}
37	
38	Fix a base field \(k\) and a smooth proper geometrically integral curve \(C\) over
39	\(k\). As in \cref{chap:Picard_LineBundlePullback}, write
40	\(\pi_T : C \times_k T \to T\) for the projection of a relative curve over a
41	\(k\)-scheme \(T\) and
42	\(\pi_T^* : \Pic(T) \to \Pic(C \times_k T)\) for the induced pullback of
43	isomorphism classes of line bundles (\cref{def:pullback_along_projection}).
44	
45	The set-valued relative Picard presheaf
46	\[
47	  \Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T) \;\;=\;\; \Pic(C \times_k T) \, / \, \pi_T^*\Pic(T)
48	\]
49	of \cref{thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined} was set up forgetting the
50	abelian-group structure inherited from tensor product. This chapter restores
51	that structure and then sheafifies. The first half (\S~REF
52	and \S~REF) verifies that the quotient is canonically
53	an abelian group and the functoriality of \cref{thm:pullback_natural} respects
54	that group structure; the result is a group-valued presheaf
55	\(\Pic^\sharp_{C/k} : (\Sch/k)^{op} \to \Ab\) on the category of \(k\)-schemes. The
56	second half (\S~REF) takes the \'etale sheafification
57	\(\Pic^\sharp_{(C/k)\et} := (\Pic^\sharp_{C/k})^\sim\) in the \'etale topology on
58	\((\Sch/k)^{op}\), and verifies that sheafification preserves the abelian-group
59	target. The \'etale-sheafified functor is the object represented by the Picard
60	scheme in \cref{chap:Picard_FGAPicRepresentability}.
61	
62	\section{Group structure on the relative Picard quotient}
63	\label{sec:relpic_group}
64	
65	The Picard group \(\Pic(X)\) of any scheme is canonically an abelian group under
66	tensor product of line bundles (Stacks tag 01CR; the inverse of \([\mathcal{L}]\)
67	is $[\mathcal{L}^{-1}] = [\mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\mathcal{L},
68	\mathcal{O}_X)]$). The pullback map
69	\(\pi_T^* : \Pic(T) \to \Pic(C \times_k T)\) of
70	\cref{def:pullback_along_projection} respects this structure: pullback of
71	\(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules sends \(\mathcal{O}_T\) to \(\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}\)
72	and is multiplicative on tensor products.
73	
74	\begin{lemma}\leanok
75	[Group structure descends to the relative Picard quotient]
76	  \label{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}
77	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.addCommGroup}
78	  \uses{def:line_bundle_on_product, def:pullback_along_projection,
79	    thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined}
80	
81	  % SOURCE: [Kleiman], "The Picard scheme", \S 2 (FGA Explained Ch.~9 \S 9.2),
82	  % Definition df:aPf + Definition df:Pfs (the absolute and relative Picard
83	  % functors are \emph{abelian-group}-valued, by construction; the relative
84	  % Picard functor is defined as a quotient \emph{group})
85	  % (read from references/kleiman-picard-src/kleiman-picard.tex, L1274-L1318).
86	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
87	  % "The {\it absolute Picard functor} \(\Pic_X\) is the functor from  the
88	  %  category of (locally Noetherian) \(S\)-schemes \(T\) to the category of
89	  % abelian groups defined by the formula
90	  %     \(\)\Pic_X(T):=\Pic(X_T).\(\)
91	  %  [\dots]
92	  %  \begin{dfn}\label{df:Pfs}
93	  %   The {\it relative Picard functor} \(\Pic_{X/S}\) is defined by
94	  %     \(\)\Pic_{X/S}(T):=\Pic(X_T)\big/\Pic(T).\(\)"
95	  \textit{Source: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', \S 2,
96	  Defs.~df:aPf + df:Pfs (the Picard functors are abelian-group-valued, and the
97	  relative one is a quotient of abelian groups; cf.\ Stacks tag 01CR for the
98	  abelian-group structure on \(\Pic\)).}
99	  Let \(C/k\) be a smooth proper geometrically integral curve and let \(T\) be a
100	  \(k\)-scheme. The Picard group \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) is an abelian group under
101	  tensor product of line bundles, and the pullback map
102	  \[
103	    \pi_T^* \;\colon\; \Pic(T) \;\longrightarrow\; \Pic(C \times_k T)
104	  \]
105	  of \cref{def:pullback_along_projection} is a group homomorphism. Its image
106	  \[
107	    H_T \;\;:=\;\; \pi_T^* \Pic(T)
108	  \]
109	  is therefore a subgroup of \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\), and the quotient set of
110	  \cref{thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined}
111	  \[
112	    \Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T) \;\;=\;\; \Pic(C \times_k T) \, / \, H_T
113	  \]
114	  inherits a canonical abelian-group structure under which the quotient map
115	  \(\Pic(C \times_k T) \twoheadrightarrow \Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T)\) is a surjective
116	  homomorphism with kernel exactly \(H_T\).
117	\end{lemma}

134	% the eventual relative-Picard carrier; Step 1's loc-triv group is what the Lean instance

138	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: Kleiman \S 2 does not supply a separate "proof" block
139	% for df:Pfs; the abelian-group structure is part of the definition (the word
140	% \emph{group} appears in df:aPf already, and df:Pfs is a quotient of the same
141	% abelian groups). The verbatim quotes for the statements above are therefore
142	% the same as for the lemma. The project-side proof is a structural restatement.
143	\begin{proof}
144	  \uses{def:line_bundle_on_product, def:pullback_along_projection,
145	    thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined, lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct,
146	    lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial, lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,
147	    lem:tensorobj_comm_iso, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,
148	    lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup, lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible,
149	    lem:pullback_unit_iso, lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv}
150	
151	
152	\leanok
153	  The construction proceeds in four steps over the locally-trivial carrier
154	  \[
155	    \mathrm{OnProduct}(\pi_C, \pi_T) \;=\;
156	      \bigl\{\, M : \text{a module on } C \times_k T \;\bigm|\;
157	      M \text{ is locally trivial} \,\bigr\}
158	  \]
159	  of \cref{def:line_bundle_on_product}, on whose isomorphism classes the
160	  relative Picard quotient is taken. It is authored \emph{in parallel} against
161	  two inputs being built concurrently: the locally-trivial pullback--tensor
162	  comparison isomorphism \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv} (consumed in
163	  Step~2 to make \(\pi_T^*\) additive) and the tensor inverse
164	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible} (consumed in Step~1 for group
165	  closure); both are themselves deferred targets.
166	
167	  \emph{Step 1 (abelian group on the carrier).} On isomorphism classes of the
168	  carrier \(\mathrm{OnProduct}(\pi_C, \pi_T)\), addition is the descent of the
169	  scheme-level tensor product of line bundles,
170	  \[
171	    [L] + [L'] \;:=\; [\, L \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}} L' \,],
172	  \]
173	  where \(L \otimes L'\) is the locally-trivial descent of the scheme-level
174	  tensor product (\cref{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}); the sum lands again in
175	  the carrier because the tensor product of two locally-trivial modules is
176	  locally trivial (\cref{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}). The zero
177	  element is the class \([\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}]\) of the structure sheaf,
178	  the monoidal unit, which is locally trivial. Associativity, commutativity,
179	  and left/right unitality of \(+\) descend from the scheme-level coherence
180	  isomorphisms --- the associator (\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}), the
181	  braiding (\cref{lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}), and the unitors
182	  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}) --- exactly as for the absolute Picard group
183	  on tensor-invertible isomorphism classes
184	  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}). The additive inverse is
185	  \[
186	    -[L] \;:=\; [\, L^{\mathrm{inv}} \,],
187	  \]
188	  where \(L^{\mathrm{inv}}\) is the witness supplied by
189	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible}: it returns a \emph{locally-trivial}
190	  module together with an isomorphism
191	  \(L \otimes L^{\mathrm{inv}} \cong \mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}\), so the inverse
192	  stays inside the loc-triv carrier and the group is closed under negation.
193	
194	  \emph{Step 2 (the pullback homomorphism \(\pi_T^*\)).} Pullback along the
195	  projection \(\pi_T\) gives an additive map
196	  \[
197	    \pi_T^* \;\colon\; \Pic(T) \;\longrightarrow\; \mathrm{OnProduct}(\pi_C, \pi_T),
198	  \]
199	  whose underlying assignment of line bundles is
200	  \cref{def:pullback_along_projection}. It is a homomorphism of abelian groups:
201	  it sends the unit to the unit, since
202	  \(\pi_T^*\mathcal{O}_T \cong \mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}\) by the unconditional
203	  pullback--unit isomorphism (\cref{lem:pullback_unit_iso}); and it is additive,
204	  since pullback commutes with tensor product on locally-trivial modules,
205	  \[
206	    \pi_T^*(N \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_T} N') \;\cong\;
207	      \pi_T^* N \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}} \pi_T^* N',
208	  \]
209	  by the locally-trivial comparison isomorphism
210	  \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv} specialised to the projection
211	  \(\pi_T\) on the \(\mathrm{OnProduct}\) carrier. This is the exact analogue,
212	  for the geometric pullback, of the additive monoid homomorphism on Picard
213	  groups induced by a ring map respecting tensor product, and is modelled on it
214	  field-for-field. Write \(H_T := \mathrm{im}(\pi_T^*)\) for its image, a
215	  subgroup of the carrier's isomorphism-class group.
216	
217	  \emph{Step 3 (setoid reconciliation).} The set-valued quotient of
218	  \cref{thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined} is taken modulo the
219	  isomorphism-class equivalence that declares two line bundles related when
220	  their classes differ by an element of \(H_T\). This relation coincides with
221	  the left-coset relation of the subgroup \(H_T\) inside the abelian group of
222	  Steps~1--2: both encode
223	  \[
224	    L \sim L' \quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad
225	      [\, L^{\mathrm{inv}} \otimes L' \,] \in H_T .
226	  \]
227	  Establishing this equivalence of relations is a concrete isomorphism-class
228	  argument over the carrier; it consumes the tensor inverse (Step~1) and the
229	  group law, but not the computational content of the comparison isomorphism.
230	
231	  \emph{Step 4 (transport).} By Step~3 the quotient set underlying
232	  \(\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T)\) is in canonical bijection with the group quotient
233	  \(\mathrm{OnProduct}(\pi_C, \pi_T) / H_T\), which carries the canonical
234	  abelian-group structure of a quotient of an abelian group by a subgroup.
235	  Transporting this structure along the bijection of Step~3 endows
236	  \(\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T)\) with its abelian-group structure, under which the
237	  canonical surjection
238	  \(\Pic(C \times_k T) \twoheadrightarrow \Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T)\) is a
239	  homomorphism with kernel exactly \(H_T\).
240	\end{proof}

242	\subsection{Helper constructions underlying the group law}
243	\label{subsec:relpic_group_helpers}
244	
245	The abelian-group instance of \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid} is assembled
246	from a handful of project-internal helper constructions on the locally-trivial
247	carrier \(\mathrm{OnProduct}(\pi_C, \pi_T)\): the tensor-product operation that
248	realises addition, the local triviality of the unit (the zero element), the
249	uniqueness of tensor inverses (which makes negation well defined), and the two
250	descended operations on isomorphism classes. Each is recorded here for the
251	one-to-one Lean correspondence; all are proved directly in Lean.
252	
253	\begin{definition}[Tensor product on relative line bundles]
254	  \label{def:rel_tensor_obj}
255	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.relTensorObj}
256	  \uses{def:line_bundle_on_product, lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct,
257	    lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}
258	  For relative line bundles \(L, L'\) on the locally-trivial carrier
259	  \(\mathrm{OnProduct}(\pi_C, \pi_T)\) of \cref{def:line_bundle_on_product},
260	  their \emph{tensor product} is
261	  \[
262	    L \,\mathbin{\otimes}\, L' \;:=\;
263	      L \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_{C \times_S T}} L',
264	  \]
265	  the locally-trivial descent of the scheme-level tensor product
266	  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}). The result again lies in the carrier
267	  because the tensor product of two locally-trivial modules is locally trivial
268	  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}). This is the operation that
269	  realises addition \([L] + [L'] := [L \otimes L']\) on isomorphism classes.
270	\end{definition}
271	
272	\begin{lemma}[The structure-sheaf unit is locally trivial]
273	  \label{lem:rel_pic_sharp_unit_loctriv}
274	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.isLocallyTrivial_unit}
275	  \uses{def:line_bundle_on_product, lem:pullback_unit_iso}
276	  The structure sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_{C \times_S T}\) (the monoidal unit) is
277	  locally trivial, hence defines an element of the carrier
278	  \(\mathrm{OnProduct}(\pi_C, \pi_T)\). Its isomorphism class is the zero
279	  element of the group of \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}.
280	\end{lemma}
281	
282	\begin{proof}
283	  \uses{lem:pullback_unit_iso}
284	  Proved directly in Lean. Local triviality is checked on an affine chart: for
285	  any point \(x\), choose an affine open \(W \ni x\); the restriction of the
286	  structure sheaf to \(W\) is its pullback along the inclusion
287	  \(W \hookrightarrow C \times_S T\), and the pullback of the structure sheaf is
288	  again the structure sheaf by the unconditional pullback--unit isomorphism
289	  (\cref{lem:pullback_unit_iso}). Composing these two isomorphisms trivialises
290	  \((\mathcal{O}_{C \times_S T})|_W \cong \mathcal{O}_W\).
291	\end{proof}
292	
293	\begin{lemma}[Uniqueness of the tensor inverse]
294	  \label{lem:rel_pic_sharp_inverse_unique}
295	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.pInverseUnique}

297	    lem:tensorobj_comm_iso, lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}
298	  Let \(M, N, N'\) be relative line bundles, and suppose there are
299	  isomorphisms
300	  \[

302	    \qquad\text{and}\qquad
303	    M \otimes N' \;\cong\; \mathcal{O}_{C \times_S T}.
304	  \]

306	  bundle is unique up to isomorphism.

309	\begin{proof}
310	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,
311	    lem:tensorobj_comm_iso, lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}
312	  Proved directly in Lean. One transports \(N\) across the chain of coherence
313	  isomorphisms
314	  \[
315	    N \;\cong\; N \otimes \mathcal{O}
316	      \;\cong\; N \otimes (M \otimes N')
317	      \;\cong\; (N \otimes M) \otimes N'
318	      \;\cong\; (M \otimes N) \otimes N'
319	      \;\cong\; \mathcal{O} \otimes N'
320	      \;\cong\; N',
321	  \]
322	  using the right unitor and left unitor (\cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}), the
323	  given trivialisations together with bifunctoriality of \(\otimes\)
324	  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}), the associator
325	  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}), and the braiding
326	  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}). This is the relative-line-bundle analogue of
327	  the standard uniqueness of inverses in the Picard group.
328	\end{proof}

331	  \label{def:rel_add}

336	  \(\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T) = \Pic(C \times_S T)/\pi_T^*\Pic(T)\) is the descent of
337	  the tensor product \cref{def:rel_tensor_obj} to isomorphism classes:
338	  \[
339	    [L] + [L'] \;:=\; [\,L \otimes L'\,].
340	  \]
341	\end{definition}

343	\begin{proof}
344	  \uses{def:rel_tensor_obj, lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}
345	  Proved directly in Lean. Well-definedness on the quotient
346	  (\cref{thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined}) is bifunctoriality of the
347	  tensor product: replacing \(L, L'\) by isomorphic representatives
348	  \(L \cong M\), \(L' \cong M'\) yields an isomorphism
349	  \(L \otimes L' \cong M \otimes M'\) (\cref{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}),
350	  so the class \([L \otimes L']\) is independent of the chosen representatives.

354	  \label{def:rel_neg}

359	  \[
360	    -[L] \;:=\; [\,L^{\mathrm{inv}}\,],
361	  \]
362	  where \(L^{\mathrm{inv}}\) is the locally-trivial tensor-inverse witness
363	  supplied by \cref{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible}, together with its
364	  trivialisation \(L \otimes L^{\mathrm{inv}} \cong \mathcal{O}_{C \times_S T}\).

367	\begin{proof}
368	  \uses{lem:rel_pic_sharp_inverse_unique, lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible}
369	  Proved directly in Lean. Well-definedness on the quotient uses uniqueness of
370	  the tensor inverse (\cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_inverse_unique}): if
371	  \(L \cong M\), then the chosen inverse witnesses \(L^{\mathrm{inv}}\) and

373	  the same class up to the isomorphism \(L \cong M\), hence
374	  \(L^{\mathrm{inv}} \cong M^{\mathrm{inv}}\), so \([L^{\mathrm{inv}}]\) is

708	  groups receiving a presheaf morphism from \(\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}\), so
709	  in particular that the abelian-group structure on
710	  \(\Pic^\sharp_{(C/k)\et}(T)\) is uniquely determined --- is recorded
711	  separately as \cref{thm:rel_pic_etale_sheaf_unit_canonical} below.
712	\end{theorem}

718	% \(\AddCommGroup \to \Set\) preserves filtered colimits, and the

738	\begin{proof}

743	\leanok
744	  The canonical morphism from a presheaf to its associated sheaf in
745	  any Grothendieck topology (Stacks tag 00WI; the canonical
746	  sheafification unit) supplies, in particular, a morphism of
747	  presheaves \(\Pic^\sharp_{C/k} \to \Pic^\sharp_{(C/k)\et}\); the
748	  hom-set of presheaf morphisms between these two objects is therefore

756	\begin{theorem}[Canonical sheafification unit with universal property (forward-looking)]
757	  \label{thm:rel_pic_etale_sheaf_unit_canonical}
758	  % \lean: TODO placeholder per DAG integrity rule 1. This is the forward-looking
759	  % universal property of the canonical étale sheafification unit; no standalone
760	  % Lean declaration realises it yet (RelPicFunctor.lean provides PicSharp.etSheaf
761	  % and etSheaf_group_structure, but not the universal-property statement), so a
762	  % placeholder name keeps the node rule-1 compliant.
763	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.TODO.rel_pic_etale_sheaf_unit_canonical}
764	  \uses{def:rel_pic_sharp, def:rel_pic_etale_sheafification}
765	
766	  % SOURCE: [Kleiman], "The Picard scheme", \S 2 (FGA Explained Ch.~9 \S 9.2),
767	  % Def.~df:Pfs + the associated-sheaf paragraph immediately following
768	  % (the \'etale sheaf is characterised as the sheaf associated to the
769	  % relative Picard functor, via the natural map from a presheaf to its
770	  % associated sheaf --- i.e.\ the sheafification unit with its universal
771	  % property)
772	  % (read from references/kleiman-picard-src/kleiman-picard.tex, L1311-L1328).
773	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
774	  % "\begin{dfn}\label{df:Pfs}
775	  %   The {\it relative Picard functor} \(\Pic_{X/S}\) is defined by
776	  %     \(\)\Pic_{X/S}(T):=\Pic(X_T)\big/\Pic(T).\(\)
777	  %  Denote its associated sheaves in the Zariski, \'etale, and fppf
778	  % topologies  by
779	  %     \(\)\Pic_{(X/S)\zar},\ \Pic_{(X/S)\et},\
780	  %     \Pic_{(X/S)\fppf}.\(\)
781	  %  \end{dfn}
782	  %  [\dots]
783	  %  if the latter functor is one of the three just displayed,
784	  %  then it is a sheaf in the indicated topology; in fact, it is the sheaf
785	  %  associated to any one of its predecessors, and the map between them is
786	  %  the natural map from a presheaf to its associated sheaf, as is easy to
787	  %  check.  In particular, the three displayed sheaves are the sheaves
788	  %  associated to the absolute Picard functor \(\Pic_X\), as well as to the
789	  %  relative Picard functor \(\Pic_{X/S}\)."
790	  \textit{Source: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', \S 2,
791	  Def.~df:Pfs together with the associated-sheaf paragraph
792	  immediately following (the natural map from a presheaf to its
793	  associated sheaf, taken inside the category of abelian groups, is
794	  universal among presheaf morphisms from \(\Pic_{X/S}\) into \'etale
795	  sheaves of abelian groups).}
796	  Let \(C/k\) be a smooth proper geometrically integral curve. There
797	  exists a canonical morphism of presheaves of abelian groups
798	  \[
799	    \eta \;\colon\; \Pic^\sharp_{C/k} \;\longrightarrow\;
800	    \Pic^\sharp_{(C/k)\et}
801	  \]
802	  with the following universal property. For every \'etale sheaf
803	  \(\mathcal{F}\) of abelian groups on \((\Sch/k)\) and every morphism
804	  of presheaves of abelian groups
805	  \(\varphi : \Pic^\sharp_{C/k} \to \mathcal{F}\), there is a unique
806	  morphism of \'etale sheaves of abelian groups
807	  \(\tilde\varphi : \Pic^\sharp_{(C/k)\et} \to \mathcal{F}\) such that
808	  \(\tilde\varphi \circ \eta = \varphi\). Equivalently, the pair
809	  \(\bigl(\Pic^\sharp_{(C/k)\et}, \, \eta\bigr)\) is initial among
810	  pairs \((\mathcal{F}, \, \Pic^\sharp_{C/k} \to \mathcal{F})\)
811	  consisting of an \'etale sheaf of abelian groups on \((\Sch/k)\) and
812	  a morphism of presheaves of abelian groups from \(\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}\)
813	  to \(\mathcal{F}\). In particular, the abelian-group structure on
814	  \(\Pic^\sharp_{(C/k)\et}(T)\) at each \(k\)-scheme \(T\) is uniquely
815	  determined (up to canonical isomorphism) by the abelian-group
816	  structure on \(\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T)\) and the universal property of
817	  \(\eta\).
818	\end{theorem}
819	
820	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: As with df:Pfs itself, Kleiman \S 2 does not
821	% supply a separate proof body --- the universal property is built
822	% into ``the sheaf associated to'' as a synonym for sheafification,
823	% as the verbatim quote above already makes plain. The project-side
824	% argument is the standard universal property of sheafification in a
825	% Grothendieck topology, transported through the colimit-preservation
826	% of \(\AddCommGroup \to \mathbf{Set}\).
827	\begin{proof}
828	
829	  In any Grothendieck topology, every presheaf has a canonical
830	  associated sheaf, and the canonical morphism from a presheaf to its
831	  associated sheaf is universal among morphisms from the presheaf into
832	  sheaves (Stacks tag 00WI; Kleiman \S 2, paragraph following
833	  df:Pfs). The \'etale site of \((\Sch/k)\) is a Grothendieck
834	  topology, so this applies in particular to \(\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}\) and
835	  yields the canonical set-valued sheafification
836	  \((\Pic^\sharp_{C/k})^{\sim_\et}\) together with the universal arrow
837	  out of \(\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}\). The forgetful functor
838	  \(\AddCommGroup \to \mathbf{Set}\) preserves and reflects the
839	  filtered colimits and finite limits used by the plus-construction,
840	  so performing the same plus-construction inside \(\AddCommGroup\)
841	  yields a sheaf of abelian groups whose underlying set-valued sheaf
842	  coincides with the set-valued sheafification; the abelian-group

843	  structure on the sheafification is transported back through this
844	  identification. The resulting morphism \(\eta\) is then by
845	  construction the universal arrow in the abelian-group-valued
846	  sheafification, which is the universal property in the form stated.
847	\end{proof}

import re
fn='blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_RelPicFunctor.tex'
lines=open(fn).read().split('\n')
# Build a brace-aware scan: track whether inside \cref{ \label{ \uses{ \lean{ \ref{ argument, and skip comments
text=open(fn).read()
# Remove comment portions (% to end of line, not escaped)
def strip_comment(line):
    out=''
    i=0
    while i<len(line):
        if line[i]=='%' and (i==0 or line[i-1]!='\\'):
            break
        out+=line[i]; i+=1
    return out
# join with newline markers but track line numbers
clean=[strip_comment(l) for l in lines]
joined='\n'.join(clean)
# find protected brace spans for \cref \label \uses \lean \ref \eqref \cref* \crefrange
prot=set()
for m in re.finditer(r'\\(cref|crefrange|cref\*|Cref|label|uses|lean|ref|eqref|mathlibok|input|texttt)\s*\{', joined):
    # find matching close brace
    start=m.end()-1
    depth=0
    i=start
    while i<len(joined):
        if joined[i]=='{':depth+=1
        elif joined[i]=='}':
            depth-=1
            if depth==0:break
        i+=1
    for j in range(m.start(),i+1):
        prot.add(j)
# now find label-shaped tokens
for m in re.finditer(r'\b(lem|thm|def|prop|sec|cor|rem|chap|df|th|ex|tag):[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_:]*', joined):
    if m.start() in prot: continue
    # compute line number
    ln=joined.count('\n',0,m.start())+1
    print(ln, repr(m.group(0)))
